title: SearXNG
description: Privacy-respecting, hackable open-source metasearch engine that aggregates results from 70+ search services without tracking or profiling users.
installation: SearXNG is a stateless metasearch engine: it stores no user search history and needs no SQL database. It does use the bundled Valkey (Redis-compatible) add-on for the optional limiter/bot-detection and caching, wired in via the SEARXNG_REDIS_URL env var (passwordless in-cluster). IMPORTANT: rotate SEARXNG_SECRET to a unique random value before going live (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`); the placeholder here must not be used in production. SEARXNG_BASE_URL is auto-filled with this app's public URL so generated links and OpenSearch descriptions are correct. Mount /etc/searxng to persist settings.yml (engines, theme, limiter, UI), which the image generates on first start; edit it there to enable/disable search engines, turn on the limiter (limiter.toml), or restrict instance access. No SMTP, S3, or external Elasticsearch is required.
